narrative.headline: What 2018 Ford Focus owners report most
narrative.summary: The dominant pattern across owner complaints is drivetrain hesitation and violent shuddering during low-speed acceleration, particularly from a complete stop through moderate speeds. A secondary cluster involves fuel system concerns, including unexpectedly low fuel economy and a gasoline odor after extended drives. Engine stalling events also appear in the data, sometimes attributed to fuel delivery components.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Owners report that accelerating from a complete stop causes the vehicle to hesitate, the engine RPM to spike rapidly, and the whole vehicle to shudder or shake violently through low-to-moderate speeds.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report the same hesitation, rapid RPM increase, and violent shuddering during acceleration from a full stop, with a subset of complaints also involving fires or crashes. · 3 fire · 4 crash · 4 injured

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: Owners report unexpectedly low fuel economy on a full tank of gasoline and a noticeable gasoline odor when the vehicle is shut off after extended driving, with some complaints involving fires or crashes. · 2 fire · 3 crash · 2 injured

issues.title: Engine And Engine Cooling
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle stalling unexpectedly while in motion, with subsequent inspection pointing to fuel delivery components such as the purge valve and fuel pump.
